p01-paris-apartment.title: Paris — The Invitation
p01-paris-apartment.objective: Inspect Sharon's changing invitation and pack for Mumbai.
p01-paris-apartment.copy: Sharon's wedding invitation has begun to erase itself. A tiny white rabbit now appears beside the route line.
dialogue: Moshé, Leah — come quickly. Leora and I are getting married in Mumbai!
Bring the invitation. Whatever happens, do not lose the Hamsa.
And a rabbit appeared in the corner of the card. Let's inspect everything.

hotspots.label: Travel map
hotspots.text: A hand-drawn route links Paris, Istanbul, Muscat and Mumbai. The direct line has been scratched out.

anomaly.text: The invitation's paper is warm, like something remembered rather than printed.

p02-paris-airport.title: Paris — Departures
p02-paris-airport.objective: Choose the valid Istanbul flight and pass the baggage check.
p02-paris-airport.copy: The direct Mumbai flight is cancelled. Only one route reaches the Muscat connection in time — and part of the boarding pass has erased itself.
dialogue: I'll compare the departure times and delays.
I'll check the terminal map. Look — the erased block on our pass is too short to say MUMBAI.
The baggage scale is broken too. Strange morning.

anomaly.text: The erased line on the boarding pass is shaped like a return code, not a destination.

p03-istanbul.title: Istanbul — The Hidden Gate
p03-istanbul.objective: Align the clocks, open the tile gate and recover the suitcase.
p03-istanbul.copy: Four clocks disagree — one shows a time from years ago. The connecting gate hides in the blue tiles, and someone has taken the green suitcase.
dialogue: Four cities, four clocks — and none agree. That one shows a Saturday night from years ago.
The tile wall is mechanical. I can feel the seams.
Excuse me — are you also looking for a green suitcase?

anomaly.text: One clock is stuck on a Havdalah hour from years before this trip.

p04-muscat.title: Muscat — Under the Stars
p04-muscat.objective: Restore the Mumbai gate with the star ceiling and assign the final seats.
p04-muscat.copy: A power failure erased the gate display. An old navigation pattern glows faintly on the ceiling — it takes two people to use it.
dialogue: I can operate the star ceiling. The mechanism still turns.
And I found the route diagram. Read me the stars — we need both pieces.

anomaly.text: The star pattern overhead shows the sky of Tel Aviv, not Muscat.

p05-mumbai-arrivals.title: Mumbai — Two Corridors
p05-mumbai-arrivals.objective: Reunite through the linked corridors, then find what Sharon left behind.
p05-mumbai-arrivals.copy: Security split them into different corridors — every control opens the other person's path. At arrivals, no one is waiting.
dialogue: I can see the power controls, but not the route.
I can see the doors, but none of them have power.
Two paths. One arrival. Work together.
Sharon isn't here. But his Hamsa is — and this photograph… two people are missing from it.

anomaly.text: Two figures in the photograph are blank white outlines.

c1s1-havdalah.title: Havdalah — The First Rabbit
c1s1-havdalah.objective: Relive the Havdalah where everything started.
c1s1-havdalah.copy: The Hamsa grows warm and Mumbai dissolves into a Saturday night years earlier. Moshé arrived early. He did not expect to see Leah again — and his English was not ready.
dialogue: (She's here. Say something. Anything. In… which language?)
You're the one from last week, right? You look like you want to say something.
I… yes. Um. Look — a rabbit! On the table!
…That is genuinely a rabbit. Okay, now we have to talk about it.

hotspots.label: Braided candle
hotspots.text: The flame leans toward the rabbit, as if pointing.

anomaly.text: The clock on the wall shows the exact hour the stuck Istanbul clock showed.

c1s2-construction.title: The Debris Hill
c1s2-construction.objective: Climb the debris hill and find the object that doesn't belong.
c1s2-construction.copy: An early not-quite-date: a construction site, a hill of debris and an abandoned house. Something on the hill is from the wrong year.
dialogue: Structurally, that debris hill is climbable. I checked.
You checked. Before or after deciding we're climbing it?

hotspots.label: Abandoned house
hotspots.text: Moshé reads the building's history from its seams; Leah reads its structure. Same house, two stories.

hotspots.label: The door, later that night
hotspots.text: The walk home ended at her door. A first kiss, given nervously — and, at the door, returned. Neither of them managed to say anything clever afterwards, which said everything.

anomaly.text: A metro card on the hill is dated years after this afternoon.

c1s3-bakery-beach.title: Bakery and the Cold Beach
c1s3-bakery-beach.objective: Time the bakery surprise, then read the footprints on the cold beach.
c1s3-bakery-beach.copy: A surprise from the bakery, and later a beach far too cold for swimming. The footprints in the sand tell an impossible story.
dialogue: One of these boxes is a surprise. Do not shake it.
These footprints arrive at the water… but none come back.
Write that down. That's the second impossible thing this week.

hotspots.label: Bakery box
hotspots.text: Inside: the exact pastry Leah mentioned once, in passing, weeks ago. He remembered.

anomaly.text: Footprints arrive and never leave.

c1s4-rooftop.title: Rooftop, Wine and Samba
c1s4-rooftop.objective: Match the skyline: what Leah sees and what Moshé sees are different layers of the same city.
c1s4-rooftop.copy: Wine, chocolate, samba from a phone speaker, and the metro construction cranes turning slowly below.
dialogue: That crane line is the new metro. Give it ten years, this skyline is unrecognizable.
The crane's counterweight is wrong for that load. Give it ten minutes.

hotspots.label: Phone speaker
hotspots.text: The samba track skips at the same beat every loop — like a memory rehearsing itself.

anomaly.text: One tower in the skyline belongs to a city they haven't visited yet.

c1s5-yarkon.title: Dinner by the Yarkon
c1s5-yarkon.objective: Cross the old bridge with the camera, the swing and the scooter intact.
c1s5-yarkon.copy: Dinner by the river. A wooden bridge with opinions, a splash that nearly took the camera, swings, and a scooter with exactly one working brake.
dialogue: The third plank is rotten. Load path goes left rail, then post.
I'll take the camera. If I fall in, the last photo will at least be dramatic.
If you fall in, hand the camera up first.

hotspots.label: The swings
hotspots.text: Two swings, still moving slightly, though no one has touched them.

hotspots.label: The quiet stretch of grass
hotspots.text: Between the splash and the swings there was a long conversation about family, and futures, and which of the two of them worries more (disputed, tied).

anomaly.text: The camera's screen briefly shows a photo they haven't taken yet: a bench at dawn.

c1s6-kibbutz.title: The Kibbutz Teddy Bear
c1s6-kibbutz.objective: Solve the settlement model together — engineer and planner.
c1s6-kibbutz.copy: A kibbutz with a giant teddy bear, and a scale model of the settlement with its pieces scattered. Leah sees structures; Moshé sees the plan.
dialogue: A settlement is a story: houses face the square, the square faces the gate.
And the water tower goes on the high point or your story ends in a drought.

hotspots.label: Giant teddy bear
hotspots.text: The bear holds a tiny paper flag with a hand-drawn rabbit on it.

anomaly.text: The model's welcome sign reads 'WELCOME HOME' in a handwriting neither of them recognizes — yet.

c1s7-sarel.title: SAR-EL in the Rain
c1s7-sarel.objective: Deduce who is missing from the group photo.
c1s7-sarel.copy: Volunteering, a rain dance that worked too well, cleaning duty, Hanukkah candles, a New Year — and a group photo with a familiar problem.
dialogue: Everyone from the base is in this photo. Count them.
Fourteen. There were sixteen of us.
The same two missing as Mumbai. Every photo, the same two.

hotspots.label: Hanukkiah in the window
hotspots.text: Eight little flames, steady in the rain-light.

anomaly.text: Two figures missing from every photograph — always the same two.

c1s8-jerusalem.title: Jerusalem of Gold
c1s8-jerusalem.objective: Retrace the day through the Old City without crossing your own path.
c1s8-jerusalem.copy: Urbex above a highway, volunteering, Jeff, AISH, the Old City at golden hour and a hostel with a roof view worth the stairs.
dialogue: Four quarters, one afternoon, and Jeff says the shortcut is 'through the second arch, obviously'.
Jeff's shortcuts have a 50% survival rating.
Which is why we map it first.

hotspots.label: Jeff
hotspots.text: Jeff insists he once saw a white rabbit on the hostel roof. Nobody believed him. You do, now.

anomaly.text: One archway opens onto a lane that, by every map, should not exist.

c1s9-meashearim.title: Shabbat in Mea Shearim
c1s9-meashearim.objective: Recount the six wrong turns and find the shape they drew.
c1s9-meashearim.copy: Mincha, cholent with strangers who became hosts, and afterwards — six wrong turns in the lamplit lanes. Drawn on paper, the turns make a shape.
dialogue: We were lost for an hour and I wasn't worried once.
Because being lost together isn't lost. Wait — draw our turns. Left, right, left…
…It makes a letter. Moshé, the wrong turns make a letter.

hotspots.label: The hosts' table
hotspots.text: Their host said: 'A guest who gets lost on the way back was meant to stay longer.'

anomaly.text: Every wrong turn bent the same way — inward, toward each other.

c2s1-athens.title: Athens — Layover Music
c2s1-athens.objective: Listen. Someone is humming a tune you know.
c2s1-athens.copy: A long layover. A musician in the corner hums a tune that doesn't belong to Athens — it belongs to Sharon.
dialogue: That tune. Sharon hums that when he's plotting something.
Sharon is always plotting something. That's his resting state.

hotspots.label: Gate screen
hotspots.text: The gate flickers between two flight numbers. One of them is not in any timetable.

anomaly.text: The musician hums Sharon's tune — a song he made up, that no stranger could know.

c2s2-paris.title: Paris at Dawn
c2s2-paris.objective: Read the street signs the way Moshé does — as one long sentence.
c2s2-paris.copy: Fresh bread, an empty avenue, the Arc de Triomphe far away, and street signs that, read in the right order, say more than directions.
dialogue: Every city is a text. Paris is just showing off about it.
Your croissant is showing off on your scarf.

anomaly.text: Read in walking order, the signs spell a question no city planner wrote.

c2s3-versailles.title: Versailles by Bike
c2s3-versailles.objective: Get both bikes home on one battery.
c2s3-versailles.copy: Rented bikes, endless gravel alleys, and one battery dying exactly halfway around the Grand Canal.
dialogue: Your battery's flat. Mine has 40%. Physics time.
Is 'physics time' where you tow me?
It's where we alternate. Towing you is plan C.

hotspots.label: Fountain
hotspots.text: The fountain's spray makes a brief arch — like a gate. You keep seeing gates.

anomaly.text: The palace clock face has no hands at all today.

c2s4-strasbourg.title: A Quiet Shabbat on the Border
c2s4-strasbourg.objective: Order the border-crossing day correctly — the timeline has been shuffled.
c2s4-strasbourg.copy: A quiet Shabbat near the German border. Afterwards, remembering the day, the events refuse to stay in order.
dialogue: We crossed the bridge before lunch. No — after the walk. No…
The memory's been shuffled. Lay the moments out and rebuild it.

hotspots.label: Border stone
hotspots.text: One foot in each country, both of them laughing about it.

anomaly.text: The remembered day plays out of order until reassembled.

c2s5-riviera.title: The Riviera Tickets
c2s5-riviera.objective: Reconstruct the Riviera route from the old tickets — and notice what all of them share.
c2s5-riviera.copy: Fishing at Antibes, the Citron Festival in Menton, a spontaneous hop across the Italian line. A pouch of old tickets holds the chapter's secret.
dialogue: Antibes, Menton, Ventimiglia… half these connections were 'wrong'.
And on every single one, our seats are adjacent. Every 'mistake', side by side.
That's not luck. That's a pattern. Write it down.

hotspots.label: Citron Festival float
hotspots.text: A float shaped like a lemon whale. No mystery. Just excellent.

anomaly.text: One ticket lists a departure city neither of them has ever been to.

c3s1-mumbai-wedding.title: The Wedding with a Missing Piece
c3s1-mumbai-wedding.objective: Order the seven blessings and discover which one is missing.
c3s1-mumbai-wedding.copy: Sharon and Leora's wedding, woven from Indian Jewish tradition: garlands, henna, the chuppah — and a booklet with one page gone.
dialogue: You made it! You look like people who followed a very strange route.
Sharon! You weren't at the airport — we found your Hamsa, and a photo, and—
Later. First: hold this booklet. Something's missing from it, and you two are the ones who'll notice what.
The blessings. There should be seven. There are six.

hotspots.label: Wedding caricaturist
hotspots.text: The caricaturist has drawn everyone — except two chairs left blank, 'for the ones still traveling'.

hotspots.label: Quiet corner
hotspots.text: Someone is peacefully asleep in the synagogue corner. Weddings are long. This is tradition too.

anomaly.text: The missing page is the blessing about building a home together.

c3s2-elephanta.title: Elephanta — The Doubles
c3s2-elephanta.objective: Compare the carved doubles from both perspectives and find who has none.
c3s2-elephanta.copy: In the cave temple, every visitor seems to have a stone double among the carvings. Leah checks the geometry; Moshé checks the faces.
dialogue: That carving is the tour guide. That one's the boat captain. It's everyone.
Not everyone. Check the two empty niches.

anomaly.text: Everyone has a stone double except the two of them.

c3s3-ahmedabad.title: Ahmedabad in Motion
c3s3-ahmedabad.objective: Catch the right train across a timetable that argues with itself.
c3s3-ahmedabad.copy: A photo with a crowd of delighted children, a temple, an ice-cold mall, and a train station where the platforms play musical chairs.
dialogue: Platform 3 says Udaipur. The board says platform 5. The conductor says 'yes'.

hotspots.label: Photo with the children
hotspots.text: Twenty children wanted one photo each. That's twenty photos. Their cheeks hurt from smiling.

anomaly.text: Platform 7 appears on the board. The station has six platforms.

c3s4-udaipur.title: Udaipur — Lakes and Fireworks
c3s4-udaipur.objective: Balance the cable car load so everyone rides before the fireworks.
c3s4-udaipur.copy: Lakes, a palace, animal shelters, a cable car with a strict weight limit, a window with the perfect view — and, that evening, an imagined wedding with horses.
dialogue: If the wedding had horses. Hypothetically. White ones.

hotspots.label: The window
hotspots.text: From this window the palace floats twice: once in stone, once in the lake.

anomaly.text: The fireworks reflected in the lake show one burst the sky never fired.

c3s5-pushkar.title: Pushkar and the Pink City
c3s5-pushkar.objective: Follow the Amber fort wall pattern to the gate before sunset.
c3s5-pushkar.copy: A camel with strong opinions, the walls of Amber climbing the hills, the honeycomb face of Hawa Mahal, and the first dust of Holi in the air.
dialogue: The wall pattern repeats every five merlons, then breaks. The break marks the path.
The camel also knows the path. The camel has done this before.
The camel is not a data source.

hotspots.label: The camel
hotspots.text: The camel regards you with the patience of a creature that has seen many tourists and respected few.

anomaly.text: The fort's shadows fall toward the sun.

c3s6-purim-holi.title: Purim Meets Holi
c3s6-purim-holi.objective: Deduce what happened to Fuzzy's tail.
c3s6-purim-holi.copy: Purim and Holi in the same week: costumes under colour clouds, a rabbi briefly on horseback, and Fuzzy the costume-dog missing his tail.
dialogue: Purim question: who took Fuzzy's tail?
Holi answer: whoever did is currently pink.

hotspots.label: Rabbi on horseback
hotspots.text: For exactly ninety seconds, the rabbi rode a horse through the courtyard. It will be discussed for years.

anomaly.text: In one photo of the party, a guest appears twice, in two costumes, at once.

c3s7-tuktuk.title: Tuk and Tuk
c3s7-tuktuk.objective: Agree the fair price and the true route.
c3s7-tuktuk.copy: Two tuk-tuks, nicknamed Tuk and Tuk. One driver quotes double and drives half the distance; the other quotes half and drives double.
dialogue: The meter, the map and basic dignity say 120.
Watch this. I learned the numbers in three languages for exactly this moment.

hotspots.label: Tuk (the first one)
hotspots.text: Tuk's dashboard shrine includes seven deities, a plastic sunflower, and — new today — a small white rabbit figurine.

anomaly.text: The driver, mid-bargain, says: 'For you, the way back is free.' There is no way back on this route.

c3s8-agra.title: Agra — Perfect Symmetry
c3s8-agra.objective: Find the single asymmetry the memory has hidden in the Taj.
c3s8-agra.copy: Jahangir's story, dawn mist, and the Taj doubling itself in the long pool. Perfect symmetry — except one deliberate flaw the memory added.
dialogue: Structure says: bilateral symmetry, near-perfect. Reflection says: liar.
The reflection has one extra window lit. The building doesn't.

hotspots.label: Guide telling Jahangir's story
hotspots.text: A story of emperors and gardens; Moshé retains every date, Leah retains how the aqueducts worked.

anomaly.text: The reflection shows one lit window that the building does not have.

c3s9-himalaya.title: Himalayan Campsite
c3s9-himalaya.objective: Set the campsite together before dark — two jobs, one order.
c3s9-himalaya.copy: A hike with a youth group, thin bright air, and a campsite to raise before dark. In a tent pocket: a letter fragment in Sharon's handwriting.
dialogue: Tent needs anchoring before the wind; fire needs shelter before the tent blocks the light.
So: my windbreak, your anchors, my fire, your guylines. Call it.
(on paper) — my gift is the journey back through yours —

anomaly.text: The stars come out before the sun has finished setting.

c4s1-bangkok.title: Bangkok by Boat
c4s1-bangkok.objective: Plot the canal boat route that hits every stop before the market closes.
c4s1-bangkok.copy: Chabad, shakshuka pizza (a fusion nobody asked for and everybody finished), a canal boat, Chinatown neon and the floating market.
dialogue: Eat first, question later. The boat leaves in four minutes.

hotspots.label: Shakshuka pizza
hotspots.text: Culinary diplomacy between two homelands. Verdict: unexpectedly excellent.

anomaly.text: One canal on the map loops back into itself — a route with no exit.

c4s2-krabi.title: Krabi — Reading the Tides
c4s2-krabi.objective: Pick the boat and hour that beat the tide.
c4s2-krabi.copy: Limestone towers, longtail boats, and a tide chart where the sea does the scheduling.
dialogue: Low tide strands the lagoon boat. High tide closes the cave mouth.
So there's a window. There's always a window.

anomaly.text: The tide chart lists two low tides at the same hour.

c4s3-kohphiphi.title: Koh Phi Phi — The Question
c4s3-kohphiphi.objective: Split the coconuts fairly — and let the evening happen.
c4s3-kohphiphi.copy: Smoothies, coconuts, a boxing match on a screen, Monkey Beach, a kayak — and at sunset, a question asked far too early, and the laughter after it.
dialogue: Okay. Hypothetically. Completely hypothetically. Would you marry me?
…We've known each other how long, and you're asking on a beach with a coconut in each hand?
The coconuts are load-bearing. They give me courage.
Ask me again someday, without the coconuts. Then it counts.

hotspots.label: Monkey Beach
hotspots.text: A monkey stole a smoothie. The monkey shows no remorse.

anomaly.text: For one heartbeat there were three shadows on the sand at sunset.

c4s4-phuket.title: Patong — The Visual Language
c4s4-phuket.objective: Navigate to the guesthouse using only pictures — no shared language.
c4s4-phuket.copy: A night street in five scripts and no common language. The route home is written in pictures: follow the signs by what they show, not what they say.
dialogue: New game: we navigate by icons only. Fish, then umbrella, then the golden bowl.
And if we end up at a fish-umbrella-bowl shop?

anomaly.text: One sign, in no language either knows, is momentarily perfectly readable.

c4s5-kohsamui.title: Passover on the Sand
c4s5-kohsamui.objective: Set the outdoor seder in the right order before Songkran's water reaches the table.
c4s5-kohsamui.copy: An outdoor Passover on the beach, and Songkran the same week: the festival of freedom meeting the festival of water.
dialogue: Seder order on one side, water festival on the other. Protect the matzah at all costs.
The matzah is under the only waterproof thing we own. My hat.

hotspots.label: Songkran water fight
hotspots.text: A city-wide water fight. Everyone loses. Everyone wins.

anomaly.text: The seder plate has a sixth space, shaped exactly like a small brass Hamsa.

c4s6-waterfall.title: The Hidden Waterfall
c4s6-waterfall.objective: Just be here. Some memories don't need solving.
c4s6-waterfall.copy: A motorbike, a wrong turn taken on purpose, and a waterfall that wasn't on any map. No puzzle here. Just the two of them, and water.
dialogue: No anomalies. No erased people. Just… a waterfall.

hotspots.label: The pool
hotspots.text: Cold, clear, perfect. The memory holds still here, unedited.

hotspots.label: The motorbike
hotspots.text: One working mirror, one optimistic engine, zero regrets.

anomaly.text: For once — nothing. A memory left completely intact is itself a message.

c4s7-ancientcity.title: The Ancient City
c4s7-ancientcity.objective: Deduce which monument the elephant will visit next.
c4s7-ancientcity.copy: A park of scaled-down monuments, rental bikes, temples — and an elephant with a fixed round it refuses to explain.
dialogue: The elephant visits the monuments in a pattern. History rewards the patient observer.
The elephant visits wherever the bananas are.

anomaly.text: One miniature monument depicts a building from a country later in the journey.

c4s8-chiangmai.title: White Temple Road
c4s8-chiangmai.objective: Sequence the mountain road past the fire haze to the White Temple.
c4s8-chiangmai.copy: A Starbucks that looks like a palace, a road smudged with forest-fire haze, a hot spring, a banana of legendary size — and the White Temple shining like frost.
dialogue: Fire haze closes the high road after noon. Hot spring detour adds an hour.
And the temple face lights at four. So the order writes itself — if we're exact.

hotspots.label: The legendary banana
hotspots.text: It's enormous. Photos were taken. Science was consulted. No conclusion reached.

anomaly.text: The temple's mirrored walls reflect the road behind you as the road ahead.

c5s1-hcmc.title: Reunification Day
c5s1-hcmc.objective: Count the flag pattern to find the parade's viewing gap.
c5s1-hcmc.copy: Flags on every balcony, a parade filling the boulevards, fireworks promised for nightfall — and one quiet gap in the crowd, if you can find it.
dialogue: Flags in runs: three red, one gold-starred banner, repeat. Except one balcony breaks the run.
The break marks the side street. That's our gap.

anomaly.text: One flag shows a symbol from no country at all — a small white rabbit.

c5s2-birthday.title: The Birthday
c5s2-birthday.objective: Seat the birthday group on the coaster within every rule.
c5s2-birthday.copy: A birthday of perfume, an amusement park, a giraffe who ate the map, a roller coaster and a waterpark. Someone has to solve the seating.
dialogue: Coaster rows of two. The birthday girl picks first. The map is inside the giraffe.

hotspots.label: The giraffe
hotspots.text: The giraffe remains unrepentant. The map remains internal.

anomaly.text: The park's clock tower counts one hour twice during the afternoon.

c5s3-hoian.title: Hoi An — Lanterns
c5s3-hoian.objective: Light the lanterns in the pattern their reflections demand.
c5s3-hoian.copy: The Japanese bridge, a river doubled with lantern light — and a tally in the notebook that suddenly makes the rabbit's pattern visible.
dialogue: Look at the Rabbit Marks list. Havdalah. The beach. The kayak. Every single one…
…is a goodbye that didn't happen. The rabbit only appears where we almost parted and didn't.

anomaly.text: One lantern's reflection glows in the river while the lantern itself is dark.

c5s4-coconut.title: Coconut Village
c5s4-coconut.copy: Round basket boats that spin like teacups with opinions. Balance is everything; dignity is optional.
dialogue: Weight low, movements opposite, and never both lean the same way.
Tell that to the boat. The boat disagrees with physics.

anomaly.text: The current carries other boats downstream; theirs drifts gently up.

c5s5-hue.title: Huế — Nested Gates
c5s5-hue.objective: Pass the nested gates in the ceremonial order.
c5s5-hue.copy: The Imperial City: gate within gate within gate. Each one must be entered by its correct door, in its correct order.
dialogue: Outer gate: east door for travelers. Middle gate: the inscription says scholars pass south.
And the inner gate hinge only turns one way. Sequence it.

anomaly.text: Looking back after each gate, the door you entered is no longer there.

c5s6-ninhbinh.title: Tam Cốc by Boat
c5s6-ninhbinh.objective: Choose the cave passage the boat can pass at this water level.
c5s6-ninhbinh.copy: Rice fields brushed gold, karst hills, and a rower steering with her feet toward three low cave mouths.
dialogue: Water's high after the rain. Clearance on the left cave is under a meter.
The rower says the middle one 'sings'. I vote for the one that sings.

anomaly.text: The middle cave hums a tune — Sharon's tune.

c5s7-sapa.title: Sapa Terraces
c5s7-sapa.objective: Count the terrace levels to find the path the mist hides.
c5s7-sapa.copy: Rice terraces stacked into the clouds, a waterfall, and the Fansipan cable car vanishing into white.
dialogue: The terraces step down in a countable rhythm. The path crosses where the rhythm breaks.
Like the flags in Saigon. The world keeps hiding doors in patterns.

anomaly.text: Through one gap in the mist: not Vietnam, but a street from Jerusalem.

c5s8-caobang.title: Cao Bằng — The Border Falls
c5s8-caobang.objective: Free the motorbike from the mud — together, in the right order.
c5s8-caobang.copy: The great border waterfall, a muddy track, and a motorbike sunk to the axle. This is a two-person extraction.
dialogue: Boards under the wheel first, or the winch just digs it deeper.
Then I pull the front while you rock the throttle. On three. Count in whichever language, but count.

hotspots.label: The border falls
hotspots.text: Two countries share one waterfall, and the waterfall has no opinion about borders.

anomaly.text: Among the mud tracks: a clean set of rabbit prints that begin and end nowhere.

c5s9-cave.title: Ngườm Ngao — The Echo
c5s9-cave.objective: Ask the cave the question, and hear what it answers back.
c5s9-cave.copy: Headlamps in a cathedral of stone. Every word comes back changed by one letter of meaning. The cave has an opinion about departures.
dialogue: It said 'stay'. Moshé. The cave said 'stay'.

anomaly.text: The echo returns 'go' as 'stay' — every time, for anyone.

c6s1-osaka.title: Osaka — Castle and Neon
c6s1-osaka.objective: Match the neon signs to their canal reflections.
c6s1-osaka.copy: A castle above its moat by day; Dotonbori's neon canyon by night. The canal reflects the signs — almost all of them faithfully.
dialogue: Six signs, six reflections. Pair them.
Five pair. The sixth reflection belongs to a sign that isn't there.

anomaly.text: One reflection in the canal has no sign above it.

c6s2-themepark.title: The Theme Park Queue
c6s2-themepark.objective: Beat the queue system with pure logic.
c6s2-themepark.copy: A day of engineered joy. The headline ride's queue splits and merges by posted rules — and one path is provably fastest.
dialogue: Left lane merges every ten minutes. Right lane is single-file but never stops.
And the express gate opens on the quarter hour. This is just a scheduling problem wearing a fun hat.

anomaly.text: The ride's photo screen shows the two of you — from a ride you haven't boarded yet.

c6s3-kyoto.title: Kyoto — Repeating Gates
c6s3-kyoto.objective: Walk the tunnel of gates, testing each with the Hamsa's eye.
c6s3-kyoto.copy: Old lanes, pagodas, the Golden Pavilion — and a tunnel of vermilion gates repeating beyond counting. Held up here, the Hamsa's eye reacts.
dialogue: Hold the Hamsa to each gate. On the true ones, the eye stays clear.
And on the false ones it clouds. Sharon gave us a compass for memories.

anomaly.text: Walking back through the gates leads deeper in.

c6s4-nara.title: Nara — The Deer Deduction
c6s4-nara.objective: Deduce which deer took the maps — yes, again with the maps.
c6s4-nara.copy: Deer that bow, stone lanterns, temple eaves — and three map pamphlets missing from a closed bag.
dialogue: The bag was closed. The maps are gone. There are four hundred deer.
Three suspects came within reach. Crumbs, timing, and one very smug deer.

anomaly.text: One deer in the park appears in a photo taken years earlier, identical antler chip and all.

c6s5-tokyo.title: Tokyo — Map Layers
c6s5-tokyo.objective: Overlay both mental maps of Tokyo to find the one crossing they agree on.
c6s5-tokyo.copy: Tower, gardens, temple, the electronics district, the great crossing. Leah's Tokyo is systems; Moshé's Tokyo is stories. The truth needs both layers.
dialogue: My map: rail lines, load, flow. Yours?
Districts, eras, names. Overlay them — where do both maps put the meeting point?
One crossing. Only one satisfies both.

anomaly.text: Both maps, overlaid, mark one street that exists on neither.

c6s6-fuji.title: Fuji — The Flower Arch
c6s6-fuji.objective: Order the day's photographs — and see what every gate photo shares.
c6s6-fuji.copy: Bikes around the lake, a garden, rice snacks, the mountain unveiled at dawn, a flower arch. Sorting the photos reveals the chapter's truth.
dialogue: Order the photos: dawn, garden, arch, lake, snacks… wait.
Every gate photo, every arch, every torii — we're always walking in. Not one photo of us leaving.
In this whole reconstruction… nobody ever leaves.

hotspots.label: Rice snack stand
hotspots.text: A small box of rice snacks. Remember this box. It will be slandered later as a ring box.

anomaly.text: In every gate photograph they are entering. There are no photographs of leaving.

c7s1-seoul.title: Seoul — Palace and City
c7s1-seoul.objective: Open the palace gates in the ceremonial order one last time.
c7s1-seoul.copy: Bikes along the Han, palace gates, borrowed traditional clothes, a city that is five centuries old and five minutes new at once.
dialogue: Last country. I can feel the journey gathering itself.
One more gate sequence. The palaces here nest like Huế — but the order runs by the compass.

hotspots.label: Traditional clothing
hotspots.text: Borrowed splendor, modestly worn; the rental shop owner approves of Leah's scarf-matching instincts.

anomaly.text: The changing-of-the-guard runs in reverse once, like a film rewinding.

c7s2-seoulmate.title: Seoulmate
c7s2-seoulmate.objective: Complete the wordplay. Receive the final fragment.
c7s2-seoulmate.copy: City lights on the water. Moshé has been building a sentence all day, syllable by syllable, and the last word is a city.
dialogue: If the next word is a pun, I'm pushing you in the river.
(She does not push him in the river.)

anomaly.text: The city's light-boards flicker, for one frame, into a word only he was thinking.

f01-page.title: Reconstructing the Page
f01-page.objective: Assemble the erased boarding pass from the chapter fragments.
f01-page.copy: Every fragment earned across eight chapters, laid out under the lamp. The erased page of the boarding pass wants to exist again.
dialogue: Eight fragments. One page. Order them by the journey, not the calendar.
And the erased block… it's a flight code. It was always a flight code.

anomaly.text: The reconstructed pass names a return flight — one seat only.

f02-thriftshop.title: The Thrift-Shop Evidence
f02-thriftshop.objective: Find the proof that the too-early question had a true intention.
f02-thriftshop.copy: A memory returns: a Jerusalem thrift shop, trying on hypothetical wedding clothes, laughing — and meaning it more than either admitted.
dialogue: We said it was a joke.
We kept the receipt, Moshé. People don't keep receipts for jokes.

anomaly.text: The receipt's date is stamped twice: once then, once now.

f03-phiphi2.title: Phi Phi — The Second Perspective
f03-phiphi2.objective: Replay the question from Leah's side of the beach.
f03-phiphi2.copy: The same sunset, remembered from the other side. From here you can see what Moshé couldn't: where the rabbit sat, and what left without him.
dialogue: From my side I could see the pier. The evening ferry — the one that connects to the airport — it left while you were mid-question.
That was my ferry. That was my flight home. I never even looked at it.
The rabbit was sitting on the pier post. Right where you didn't look.

anomaly.text: In this memory, Moshé's shadow points at the pier even though he never faced it.

f04-proposals.title: Six Possible Proposals
f04-proposals.objective: Test each proposal place with the Hamsa and reject the red herrings.
f04-proposals.copy: Paris. The Taj. Koh Phi Phi. The Vietnamese waterfall. Fuji. Seoul. Six places float like postcards. Each holds one authentic clue — or one beautiful lie.
dialogue: Hamsa up. Test them all. Paris ring-mark?
Clouds over. Coffee stain, like we thought. Fuji ring box?
Clouds. Rice snacks. The eye only clears on Phi Phi… and on Seoul.

anomaly.text: Four of the six postcards cloud the Hamsa's eye.

f05-metapuzzle.title: The Missing Flight
f05-metapuzzle.objective: Order the fragments, choose the true memories, rebuild the ticket — and answer.
f05-metapuzzle.copy: Everything converges. The fragments align, the false memories fall away, the boarding pass completes itself — except for one line, which only they can write.
dialogue: The missing flight was never on our route. It was your return flight — the one you never boarded, after a question asked too early.
Sharon knew. The rabbit marked every goodbye we skipped. The Hamsa kept the true memories safe until we could read them.
So the last line of the ticket isn't a destination. It's what you said instead of boarding.

anomaly.text: The final line of the boarding pass awaits three words.

f06-ending.title: The Bench at Dawn
f06-ending.objective: Sit. Watch the sun rise on the stage of your story.
f06-ending.copy: A bench at dawn. The rabbit, unhurried at last. A teddy bear, a lizard, a drink with two straws, the brass Hamsa warm in the middle. Behind them, every skyline of the journey at once — the landscapes that became the stage for their story.
dialogue: (voice, warm) Told you my gift wasn't the kind you unwrap.
Every landscape we crossed… none of it was the destination.
It was the stage. The story was always us.
Ask me again someday, you said. Without the coconuts.

hotspots.label: The bench
hotspots.text: There is room for two, and the rabbit.

hotspots.label: The skyline of everywhere
hotspots.text: Tel Aviv into Paris into Mumbai into Bangkok into Hoi An into Kyoto into Seoul. One horizon.

hotspots.label: A date kept like a stone in a pocket
hotspots.text: 14/01/2025. The day the certainty was said out loud: wherever the day goes — whatever the weather, the hour or the plan — the shared future is the one fixed point.

anomaly.text: No anomalies remain. The memory is whole.
